original_abstract:
SimpleITK is a streamlined interface for the Insight Segmentation and Registration Toolkit (ITK), specifically engineered to accelerate rapid prototyping, education, and research through high-level programming languages. While ITK is a powerful, template-based C++ library renowned for its generic and extensible framework in biomedical image processing, its traditional interface requires navigating a complex hierarchy. Historically, ITK relied on WrapITK to provide access to languages like Python and Tcl; however, WrapITK directly exposed ITK’s intricate template structures, which can be daunting for many users.

In contrast, SimpleITK provides an intuitive, simplified abstraction layer. It replaces the complex, demand-driven pipeline of ITK with straightforward procedural methods and a template-less architecture. To further enhance the user experience, SimpleITK includes practical features such as binary distribution packages and overloaded operators. This design represents a deliberate shift away from direct code wrapping in favor of a facade-based structure that exposes essential functionality while concealing internal complexity. By leveraging manual filter descriptions, automated code generation, and sophisticated C++ metaprogramming, SimpleITK makes the robust capabilities of ITK accessible to a much broader scientific audience. SimpleITK is released as an open-source library under the Apache License 2.0; documentation and downloads are available at http://www.simpleitk.org.
humanized_abstract:
SimpleITK provides a lightweight interface to ITK, to facilitate rapid development, learning and science in high level languages such as Python, C#, Java, and R. ITK is a highly generic, extensible C++ class library for performing registration and segmentation on biomedical images. The traditional interface of ITK results in a very deep namespace hierarchy. ITK has traditionally used WrapITK to allow Python, and Tcl, to access ITK algorithms and classes. However, since WrapITK maps out the template code of the ITK classes and algorithms, this can be quite intimidating to the average user.

More sophisticated libraries such as ImageIO or ITK implement very complex behavior. An example of this complexity is the demand-driven execution model of the ITK pipeline which requires a deep understanding of the underlying algorithms and can be very hard to manage in simple use cases. SimpleITK defines a much simpler facade around those underlying algorithms by introducing a set of simple procedures. Most of the filter implementation is automatically generated from filter descriptions and C++ metaprogramming is heavily used to provide to the user a simple and safe interface to complex imaging processing while making the full strength of the underlying filters available to the advanced user. SimpleITK is an Open-Source C++ Library under the Apache License 2.0 (http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0) and more information about it and its distribution can be found in http://www.simpleitk.org.
